title: Allow Temporary Passwords with Immediate Forced Change
description: Allow temporary password use for system logons with an immediate change to a permanent password following initial use.

checks.name: New Accounts Require Password Change at First Logon
checks.purpose: Verify newly created or reset accounts have the Must change password at next logon flag set, preventing continued use of temporary credentials

checks.name: New Accounts Require Password Change at First Logon
checks.purpose: Verify newly created or reset accounts in AD have the Must change password at next logon flag set

checks.name: No Accounts Have Passwords Set to Never Expire Without Justification
checks.purpose: Confirm no standard user accounts have the Password never expires flag set without documented justification

checks.name: New Accounts Are Provisioned with Expired Passwords
checks.purpose: Verify new accounts are created with passwd --expire or chage -d 0 to force an immediate password change at first logon

checks.name: Password Expiration is Set for All Interactive Accounts
checks.purpose: Confirm no interactive accounts have a maximum password age of 99999 (never expires) in /etc/shadow

checks.name: New Accounts Are Provisioned with Expired Passwords
checks.purpose: Verify new accounts are created with chage -d 0 to force an immediate password change at first logon on Debian

checks.name: Password Expiration is Set for All Interactive Accounts
checks.purpose: Confirm no interactive accounts have a maximum password age of 99999 in /etc/shadow on Debian

remediation: Set the Must change password at next logon flag on all newly created or reset accounts. Audit accounts with Password never expires and document justification. On Linux, use chage -d 0 when provisioning new accounts to expire the password immediately, forcing a change at first login.
